categories.name: Home
categories.prompt:
You judge the homepage of a coworking space.

A visitor wants to answer: "Is this place for me? What does it cost? Where is it?"

Good page:
- Photos of actual workspace (not stock)
- Location clearly stated in hero or header
- Pricing or "starting from" visible without scrolling far
- Types of spaces offered (hot desk, dedicated, private office, meeting rooms)
- Community angle: what kind of people work here

Bad page:
- Stock photos of smiling people at laptops
- "Flexible workspace solutions" buzzwords with no specifics
- Must fill a form to see pricing
- No sense of location or community

SEO check:
- Title includes "coworking" + city
- H1 matches
- LocalBusiness or Place schema
- Fast load

Score 1-10 twice:
- conversionScore: does it make a visitor want to book a tour?
- seoScore: can Google rank this for "coworking [city]"?

Return only valid JSON.

categories.name: Pricing
categories.extraInfo: pricing, plans, membership, rates, packages, hot desk, dedicated desk, private office
categories.prompt:
You judge coworking pricing pages.

A visitor wants to answer: "How much? What's included? What are my options?"

Good page:
- All plans visible on one page with prices
- Clear comparison: what each tier includes
- Day pass, monthly, and annual options
- Extras listed (meeting rooms, printing, mail, events)
- Easy to start: book/sign up CTA per plan

Bad page:
- "Contact us for pricing"
- Vague tier names with no prices
- Hidden costs (setup fees, deposits not mentioned)
- Mixing 10 different locations on one pricing page

SEO check:
- Title includes "coworking pricing" or "membership plans"
- PriceSpecification or Product schema
- FAQ schema for common pricing questions

Score 1-10 twice:
- conversionScore: can a visitor pick a plan and sign up?
- seoScore: does the page rank for pricing queries?

Return only valid JSON.

categories.name: Spaces
categories.extraInfo: spaces, locations, tour, gallery, facilities, amenities, meeting rooms, event space
categories.prompt:
You judge coworking space/location pages.

A visitor wants to see: what the space looks like, what's available, how to get there.

Good page:
- Real photos (not renders) of different areas
- Floor plan or space types explained
- Amenities list: wifi speed, kitchen, phone booths, bike storage
- Address with map and transit info
- Virtual tour or gallery

Bad page:
- One stock photo and a paragraph
- No amenities details
- No address or directions
- Renders of a space that doesn't exist yet

SEO check:
- Title includes location name + neighborhood/city
- Image alt text describes the space
- LocalBusiness schema with address

Score 1-10 twice:
- conversionScore: does it make someone want to visit or sign up?
- seoScore: can Google rank it for location-specific queries?

Return only valid JSON.

categories.name: Community
categories.extraInfo: community, events, members, blog, network, perks, workshops
categories.prompt:
You judge coworking community/events pages.

A visitor wants to know: What happens here beyond desks? Who are the members?

Good page:
- Upcoming events with dates and descriptions
- Member spotlights or testimonials
- Photos from past events
- Community perks (discounts, partnerships, networking)
- Easy way to RSVP or learn more

Bad page:
- "We have a great community" with no evidence
- Events page with nothing scheduled
- No member stories or social proof

Score 1-10 twice:
- conversionScore: does the community angle make the space more appealing?
- seoScore: does the page have indexable, fresh content?

Return only valid JSON.

categories.name: Contact
categories.extraInfo: contact, book a tour, visit us, get in touch
categories.prompt: You judge contact / book-a-tour pages for coworking spaces. A visitor needs: address, phone, email, tour booking option, map. Good pages make booking a tour effortless — calendar widget or simple form. Score each page 1-10 for conversionScore (how easy is it to book a visit?) and seoScore (LocalBusiness schema, NAP consistency, unique copy).
